title: Chicken Pot Pie
description: The classic — tender chicken and vegetables in a creamy sauce under a golden, flaky crust. Leftover chicken becomes Sunday supper.
instructions: Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the onion and carrots. Cook for about 5 minutes, until the onion is translucent and the carrots begin to soften.
Stir in the salt and rosemary (if using).
Sprinkle in the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Slowly whisk in the chicken broth and heavy cream until smooth.
Stir in the cooked chicken, frozen peas, and parsley (if using). Remove from the heat.
Line a pie dish with one pie crust.
Pour the filling into the crust.
Place the second crust on top, seal and crimp the edges, and cut a few slits in the top for venting.
Brush the top with beaten egg if desired.
Bake for 35 to 45 minutes, until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbling.
Let rest for 10 to 15 minutes before serving.
notes: Frozen mixed vegetables can be substituted for the carrots and peas.
Cover the crust edges with foil if they brown too quickly.
Let the pie rest before slicing so the filling can set.
leftovers: Good leftovers — keeps 3 to 4 days in the refrigerator, and the crust re-crisps in the oven.
storage: Cover and refrigerate up to 4 days, or freeze up to 3 months (baked or unbaked). Reheat at 350°F until hot and the crust is crisp.
whyWeChoseThis: Turns leftover or rotisserie chicken into a Sunday-supper centerpiece.
One pie feeds six to eight and freezes beautifully.
The kind of comfort food that brings everyone to the table.

editorialNotes.note: Added to the cookbook — the classic chicken pot pie, leftover chicken to Sunday supper.
